records.ety: [L. determinans, p. pr. of determinare: cf. F. dterminant.]

defs: Serving to determine or limit; determinative.
That which serves to determine; that which causes determination.
The sum of a series of products of several numbers, these products being formed according to certain specified laws
notes: thus, the determinant of the nine numbers a, b, c,a, b, c,a, b, c, is a b c - a b c + a b c] - a b c + a b c. The determinant is written by placing the numbers from which it is formed in a square between two vertical lines. The theory of determinants forms a very important branch of modern mathematics.

defs: A mark or attribute, attached to the subject or predicate, narrowing the extent of both, but rendering them more definite and precise.
